title: gardenia archboldiana
intro: Gardenia archboldiana is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the gardenia family, native to Sri Lanka. It is traditionally used in medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The plant is said to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and providing antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of Gardenia archboldiana often involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves or flowers, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for medicinal purposes.
